reviews.review_text:
Family friendly and quiet caravan park. Lots of trees and gardens, not a concrete village park like others.

Close to the beach and walking distance to local sports club and Sunday markets.

We were on powered site 166. South facing which is good for afternoon sun not blaring in the door.

Friendly and helpful staff. Fantastic check-in parking area - obviously designed by caravan users. Number plate reader gate makes it easy to get in and out.

Shaded & heated pool with kiddy pool. Basket ball and volley ball courts. Very clean and tidy camp kitchen.   Watch the Curlews at play, fish in the adjoining creek. Ensuite sites are great too.

Power and water sites. All flat. Dump point private and tidy.

We’ll be back for sure!

We have stayed many times at Pottsville North and absolutely love it here. The kids thoroughly enjoy themselves every time with the heated swimming pool. Loads of space for bigger vans which is great for us
We loved our stay at Pottsville North. The park amenities are nice and clean and the sites are big and not cramped. We will definitely be back!
Had a lovely stay, such a perfect cabin in an amazing spot

Have been coming to pottsville caravan pk since 2004. We had vans etc but the last 8yrs have been staying in the cabins. They are clean and have been updated over time. Now they have glamping tents and I love them. Roomy and every thing you need.. will stay in them again. Management are friendly and helpful.
This caravan park is very well kept & a great place to spend days laying around or walking around. The staff are fantastic & very friendly & helpful.

Stayed at potty north now I think4 times and absolutely loved every time!
Staff are so helpful and friendly!!
Love it’s a  stone's throw from the beach and small walk to the creek!
Love hearing the birds and wildlife all day long and riding my bike to the beach and to town!!

Beautiful grounds, helpful staff, facilities always clean, great location walking distance to beach, creek, shops and cafes, heated pool is excellent in the cooler months, we have had a lot of wonderful stays at Pottsville north caravan park over the years. I highly recommend.

Stayed long term in our caravan with my family.
Park is quiet, clean, well maintained, child friendly and has lovely present staff always available to help.
Location is fantastic, right in the middle of everything Pottsville has to offer but when your in the park it feels peaceful, surrounded by the lovely trees, gardens and beautifully maintained grounds.
Would highly recommend for a family holiday, long term stay or little weekend getaway.

First camping trip for our family, we had a great time. Very clean, wildlife was amazing, spent plenty of time in the pool and relaxing.
Great trip, will definitely come back!

We stayed in our van . Cant felt this park Amenities very clean and serviced. Id like to return soon . Grounds are very well maintained. Family cabins we had a van though had a quick look. Booking was easy over the phone, not complicated. Back soon

Top place for tent camping. Place was very clean and well looked after. The staff were very friendly and communicated brilliantly before our arrival and during our trip. I will be back for more trips soon.
Its very close to town, beach and the river.
There is a sports club really close to the site.
The campsite was very well drained and held up in the rain. Great park.
Could not fault gr8 place to stay
Quiet campsite, good facilities.

We stayed 4 nights on a powered site, just across from the pool and beach volleyball court.

The pool area was great and had a pool suitable for little ones. The area includes showers near the gates to rinse yourself off as well.

The amenities were clean and handy to the site. It's only a minute or two to the main street of Pottsville were you can grab a coffee. There was a fish van that turned up on a couple of occasions at the front of the park as well.

The staff in the office were great and very helpful. You provide your vehicle rego and the camera at the gate reads your plate and in you go. No need for codes. However, you will need a code for the amenities.

The cost of the site was very reasonable and definitely stay at the park again.
Clean.quite park. amenities clen

Stayed for one night camping out of our car and honestly one of the best caravan parks we have visited. Some of the caravan rentals look a bit old but we haven't seen the inside BUT all the amenities were super clean, modern enough, plenty to go around and well placed. Camp kitchen had everything you need, spots were big enough as well. Pool was good and clean, staff was friendly and even the water on the sites had a good taste. Quite camping only disadvantage I could think off is that a brush turkey took some bread from our vehicle and we found them on our table twice, but that's obviously not on the camping.
Great place, would recommend.
Very accommodating staff and very helpful. Large van sites. Quiet place to stay. We stayed overnight on our way north. Reasonable charges.
We had a great weekend here, very clean camp grounds and the location is perfect. Shout outs to Jess and Matthew for being so welcoming and helpful.

.. we just had 2 wonderful nights at this beautiful peaceful, clean & relaxing water front holiday park .. very helpful, happy & cheerful staff .. the grounds were immaculate .. the kitchen & ablution blocks were very clean ..  highly recommend this gem 💎🩵🌊⛱️
Relaxing 😌

Spent the long weekend there and ended up booking for a extra night, the lady who was at reception on Monday morning was absolutely amazing she helped us with the washers and dryers highly recommended and we will definitely be booking a longer stay very soon.
Fabulous spot. Lovely clean amenities. Peaceful

This park is south pottsville....we are waterfront and it is amazing. Fantastic spot for families as the river runs out to sea approximately 150mtrs away. Great walking tracks to the beach which is just across the bridge. Toilet/shower block super clean. There is a separate accessible toilet shower room. Very quiet here.
Shops are across the road. Most places are easy to walk to.
The whole park is very well maintained.  It is a small park. Will definitely be back.

Beautiful location on the creek and with the surf beach only a short walk away.|We stayed in a waterfront 2-bedroom cabin which was a decent size for our family of 4.  The cabin was furnished nicely and was maintained well by the cleaners.|Since we stayed for a week over the NY holidays, it was busy as expected but it's also not a large holiday park so it never felt crowded.|Shops and restaurants were only a short walk away.  There's also a tavern and sports club within walking distance as well.|Check-in was quick and easy, staff welcoming and friendly.|A week was probably a bit too long for us as there isn't that much to do around the area.  But if you're looking for a place that's beautiful and allows you to just relax then this would be a great choice.|Our family spent most of our time swimming and paddleboarding in the clear creek right in front of our cabin.
Great place to stay.
Loved the convenience of this park. We went for 4 nights and didn’t move the motorhome once. We could walk to the beach, creek, shops and restaurants. Great place to stay.
Love this spot, great view and quiet

Whilst this is a great location on the creek and walking distance from shops etc, it’s the most expensive site we’ve ever paid for and never will again. |The showers need a decent clean including the roof as mouldy, the washing machines weren’t working and there’s no pool or playground.|We’ve paid less and stayed in better.

This park is in a great location, on the creek which is a great place for young children to swim. Also just a short walk to town.  The sites are in a loop, so be aware that arriving at 12.30 meant we had 3 caravans to park up in front of us so it was a 30+minute wait, since we were on the 2nd last site (at the exit gate) Also unhitching and hitching up means traffic exiting park waits whilst this process takes place unless you have a small van or a camper. Staff are friendly, we had a slow leak in one of the vans tyres and the groundsman checked in that we were ok and had everything we needed. Loved our time there.

The best spot to enjoy precious time right on moobal creek 500m from the ocean.
Beautifuly presented water view units, grounds very well kept.
Caravan park is number plate gated.
Heaps of green space right on the water. So much birdlife moving throughout the day and stone curlews at night.
Just park your car and just walk 2 mins into the small town of Pottsville that has it all!
Great spot to base yourself to ride your bikes through Hastings Point on to Casurinas big pathway past many dune entries to beaches and onto Kingscliff.
Only 15mins druve to Northern rivers beautiful rail trail passing through Moobal where you can park safely and wheel up to ride the recently finished trail to Murwillumbah.
Take your kayaks or sup and enjoy this lovely waterway that flows into the Pacific ocean.

The location is fantastic and I would highly recommend for young families.  The sound of the ocean at night was very relaxing, water access metres away, a bottle shop,butcher, eatery’s and amazing IGA 300m walk up the road.  The blonde lady on reception went over and above to accommodate our needs and had exceptional customer service. The groundsman cleaned the leaves off out site before we set up.  We highly recommend.
Had a lovely night here! The reception lady was very nice and helped me book a late check-in over the phone. The amenities were clean and well-maintained.

The campsites were great all double slabs, we could walk over to the street, there’s cafes, newsagent fish & chip shop, IGA Supermarket plenty more.  The staff in the park were great and the boom gate to get in &out recognised our number plate which was so easy.  Would recommend the park 100%
Visiting family staying there on a caravan site.  Great spot for fishing and safe swimming for children

All good except band nobody older kids screaming an yelling atv6.30!,pm was. Most annoying...phoned reception only to be transferred to other staff ,4:,times....not good and no staff attending....I know it's a holiday park but noise totally unexceptable

Well run, priced, and maintained. Good sized sites so didn't feel like a sardine. Friendly check in, loved the licence plate recognition boom gates. Adequate and clean amenities. Felt like a guest rather than a commodity and will gladly stay again next time I pass through.

Amazing van park, impeccably maintained,amenities and grounds.Friendly helpful staff.Please do yourself a favour if you are travelling up/ down the coast and stop here.Bird life abundant,beach 4 min walk,drive to shops. Oh the serenity!!!🤗👍

What a nice park. Check in was smooth and the guy in reception very nice and accommodating. We’re just travelling through so only staying one night and he was considerate by allowing us to remain hitched as the park is quiet at the moment. The boom gate has number plate recognition which makes it so much easier to enter and exit. It’s a nice location near the river. The spark is very well maintained. My only negative would be that the wifi is patchy and even my own internet reception is quite bad. I would definitely stay here again (and bring my Starlink 😆).
Very good facilities, the garden cabin was well appointed and clean.

The location is great, few meters away deom the beach. Our cabin is clean and has the basic amenities that you need. The only thing is our bed is not made up and we need to do it ourselves. Otherwise all is good

Great welcome by Park staff.
Fabulous location.
Very very clean Ameneties.

Great caravan park
Glenn Rowe auto electrician.
Great caravan Park, friendly staff , every relaxing

We stayed at Pottsville North over the May Day long weekend. The first day was excellent, but days two and three were ruined by kids playing cricket toward our site from the basketball court, repeatedly hitting our caravan and car at speed. Despite approaching management four times and being told that they shouldn't be playing cricket by the person on the front desk, apparently the only “solution” was having the kids face the other way to play despite the park being largely empty with a grassed area near the volleyball court that could have been utilised. ||||The balls continued to fly into our site and hit a tent and another car in the opposite direction. After the ball hit our BBQ while we were cooking dinner, my husband threw that ball into the bush. Then one of the intoxicated fathers stormed onto our site and verbally abused him in front of our two young daughters.||||Rob, the on-site manager, witnessed the harassment and suggested we could move our car which would have put it where we wouldn't be able to see it. He spoke to us and the other family and told the feral bogans to move it along. However the family continued to taunt and harass us all evening after management left. The kids returned the next morning to play cricket and again at 9 pm to deliberately bounce their basket ball out the front of our site.||||Another customer on another site backed up our version to the manager but it fell on deaf ears. ||He had been at the park for 5 weeks and said this group was shocking. Also hitting his vehicle and van with a rugby ball numerous times.||||We checked out first thing the following morning.||||After we complained, Melita (another manager) blamed us for not reporting it sooner and offered only a one-night refund, despite photos I’d sent showing the kids playing cricket on Sunday. This park is far too small for cricket, and management’s response was woefully inadequate. We will not be returning.||||There were a number of the parks own policies that were blatantly broken and ignored by the staff running the site.||||This one star review is based on our experience.

We stayed at Pottsville North over the May Day long weekend. The first day was excellent, but days two and three were ruined by kids playing cricket toward our site from the basketball court, repeatedly hitting our caravan and car at speed. Despite approaching management four times and being told that they shouldn't be playing cricket by the person on the front desk, apparently the only “solution” was having the kids face the other way to play despite the park being largely empty with a grassed area near the volleyball court that could have been utilised.

The balls continued to fly into our site and hit a tent and another car in the opposite direction. After the ball hit our BBQ while we were cooking dinner, my husband threw that ball into the bush. Then one of the intoxicated fathers stormed onto our site and verbally abused him in front of our two young daughters.

Rob, the on-site manager, witnessed the harassment and suggested we could move our car which would have put it where we wouldn't be able to see it. He spoke to us and the other family and told the feral bogans to move it along. However the family continued to taunt and harass us all evening after management left. The kids returned the next morning to play cricket and again at 9 pm to deliberately bounce their basket ball out the front of our site.

Another customer on another site backed up our version to the manager but it fell on deaf ears.
He had been at the park for 5 weeks and said this group was shocking. Also hitting his vehicle and van with a rugby ball numerous times.

We checked out first thing the following morning.

After we complained, Melita (another manager) blamed us for not reporting it sooner and offered only a one-night refund, despite photos I’d sent showing the kids playing cricket on Sunday. This park is far too small for cricket, and management’s response was woefully inadequate. We will not be returning.

There were a number of the parks own policies that were blatantly broken and ignored by the staff running the site.

This one star review is based on our experience.

We had a great experience. A very generous sized site for what we needed. The ammenities were convenient and clean. The basket ball hoop was good for a fun bit of exercise and then, the ability to cool off in the pool just topped it all off. We’d booked for 3 nights but ended up staying another extra night. A short walk to town where you can get almost everything you’d need, or the same to the beach which was quiet but still recovering from the recent storms. We will definitely come back and would love to use the same site number again. Thanks team
We stay there a lot. Great friendly staff. Near the club. We will be back again and again!

Recently stayed for a week, overall was good but there were a few things I thought I’d mention. Everyone was friendly and respectful and was a kid friendly park.
- disabled amenities were needing an overhaul, barely any water pressure from the damaged shower head.
- a kids change table would have been amazing in the bathrooms.
- advertised with WIFI but was not available at the back of the park.
-bathrooms were nice and clean/tidy however the doors were very loud at all hours of the morning and night when staying across from them.
-heavy lighting at night, couldn’t leave camper open for air flow.
- we were told it was a 7 night minimum but it was clear other guests were only staying for 2/3 nights each.

Would revisit!

I stayed 4 nights in a garden cabin recently. There were ants running all over the kitchen bench, a couple in the kettle when I made tea and also running over my bed. The cabin is looking quite shabby. I have stayed there numerous times but now prefer the one in town.
Great place to Holiday swimming .fishing.plent6 of park space for kids to play and right in town as well
Gr8 spot

Overall I found this place quite nice to visit. There's lots of wandering around you can do. The water is adjacent to the park and shops are very close. However have couple things to note. #1 I had ensuite powered site. Yes it's nice to have facility very close to me but I do not like a bright shining light at night into my caravan area until morning. Also the only fridge/freezer in camp kitchen area. Even though empty, absolutely stank of prawns. Telling maintenance guy coming through, he just says, yeah, they put in crabs and everything. He just cleaned up around kitchen and left fridge as is.
If you want to offer such facilities, may I suggest a separate thing for stinky seafood or ban that in your fridge.
As it stank out the ice blocks stored during my stay.
Both contacts I had with staff in park were just accepting it as is. And not working on feedback.
Yes it's good for location but it lost points with me with things that could be remedied but just accepted that's the way it is.
Is it a good place to go. Yes. Would I personally come back though, no, unless those points were amended.
Great spot ,right on the water fantastic for kids,fishing ,having drinks & nibbles ln the afternoon ,great patrons,clean & tidy.
What an awesome  caravan park  , short walk to the  local  shops ,  will definitely  be  back

Great caravan park .helpful staff.
Glenn Rowe mobile Auto electrician.
Pottsville is a nice little town and the caravan park is so close to shops & coffee.
Stayed on a powered site. Park is close to shops and a not too far walk to to al club. Park clean and backs onto creek/inlet. Staff friendly
Great local spot with direct access to beach

Great location. Cabins are located on the banks of Mooball Creek and are comfortable and contain all the amenities required for a relaxing holiday. Park is close to shops, local tavern and sports club. Staff are very friendly and happy to  assist in any way they can.

We had a wonderful Easter holiday at Pottsville South.  Very family friendly, lived the updates of the day on my phone. We will be back. Loved the birds, kookaburras for the early morning song. Thank you

Such a rip off! Instead of being honest and saying there were no campsites left when I rang we were given a piece of dirt beside a concrete slab. Charged $230 for two nights!!! To pitch a two man tent!!! When I queried they said it was double due to school holidays!!! Nowhere on their website is this indicated!! Never ever again!
Wow a great place
WiFi is so weak it's unusable throughout entire park. Im unable to work. It should not be advertised as being provided. Very misleading.
Great park waterfront everything you want within walking distance

Beautiful caravan park ideal for any person
Water way swimming was lovely
Park was spotless clean in facilities and very easy location for the little town
Park management kept us up to date via text and messages we may need to make our stay easy
C u again soon

We had the pleasure of staying at the van park for 4 weeks on the ensuite and tent site. The sites are large, amenities spotless and the staff so helpful. Due to unforeseen circumstances we had to change our booking several times and the staff were so helpful. The wildlife is amazing particularly the family of Curlews. We loved that its dog free. We will be back. Thank you
Beaut park with mature trees grassy sites and lovely pool. Not a great location for beach.
Stayed for 2 nights nice and quite loved the pool and cabin  was great
Can't wait to escape Brisbane and stay here! Love this place! Ensuites sites are perfect!
Couldn’t have helped us more. They fitted us in for a week and the cabin was amazing. Would recommend to anyone.
Quiet little seaside spot. Well maintained park.

This is a hidden gem! The park is beautiful! So clean and tidy, the staff are the friendliest we have ever experienced. Donna in reception was awesome! The manager Robert was great! Such a nice chilled guy, nothing was too much trouble. The weather was a little cooler but the heated pool was delightful!

Great spot and friendly, helpful staff.
Walking distance to bowlo and shops.
Sites can be tight, but worth the effort.

Powered camp
Site was perfect! Nice shade from tree and close to amenities. Staff very friendly and helpful highly recommend
Nice quiet town, nice quiet carapark. Fix your damn camp kitchen fridge! There is a reason no one uses it.

Dreadful amenity’s  The rooms were tiny we were given one tiny white towel each to last for 4 days . It rained and there was no way to keep the floors dry or clean. We asked for new towels and were given a bathmat the size of a facecloth. No fresh towels were offered.The pillows were hard and the place overpriced for what you get. Only one aircon unit so the place was stuffy and very hot. The swimming pool was so over chlorinated it made our kids eyes  red for a week after.  Definitely do not recommend

Clean Amenities,friendly Office,food trucks supplied a varity of food on 3 of the nights we were here,Shops and Cafe's only a 15 min walk,Sports Club 7 min walk and excellent food.|Locality of Park is great and they took the time to accommodate the kids with games plus a great swimming pool.

Staying on a power camp site. Facilities always clean. Lots of wildlife. Surrounding area is awesome catching fish everyday got a jack , Taylor and a few flathead. Peaceful place to come and get away.
Will be back
Park grounds maintained but sites  need upgrading. Has pool and is short walk to beach and shops

Used the powered site for a caravan. What a beautiful surprise, nestled next to the Mooball Creek and a short easy walk to Pottsville Beach, this park is a gem. The friendliest of staff, clean and modern amenities and tidy common areas. We already booked in for next year. BIG 5 STARS 🌟 🌟🌟🌟🌟

We have mixed feelings about our recent stay at Pottsville South caravan Park. We stayed on site PT12 which is located near the ammenities block which was very clean before the park got busy, it is right on the road which was very noisy from 5am to late at night. After the park got busy the kids had no respect for anyone, running through your site and very noisy until late at night. We don’t mind kids having fun but they could have had a bit more respect for fellow campers.

The major drawback for the site we were on was the infestation of Stink beetles. We notified the office and were told that they were normal and that there was nothing they could do. Someone came and offered us a spray bottle with soapy water or a can of fly spray, which we had already bought and ended up using the whole can. We ended up leaving a day early as we couldn’t stand them anymore. We spoke to a couple of other campers who had stayed on this site before (last year and before) they said that they were there then. We have now unpacked everything at home and we have brought them home with us to a different state where we don’t have any problems with them. We cleaned up all the dead bugs each day and the attached photos are just a small amount of the bugs.

We feel that the management should do something to fix this problem ASAP.
Great location for holidays.  It's clean and well maintained.  We will definitely go back.

We just checked the availability for a small van site at Pottsville South , we have stayed here many times and found the park to be in good order and clean .
So we thought okay lets  book in for 4 nights  in the middle of November (mid week ) no school holiday's, no public holidays days, mid week . $90.00 per night !! What a joke what a rip off , I can’t believe this price what are they  up to? Council or management are gouging  is what they are doing , I really don’t think that they can justify these prices.
No wonder the grey nomads are traveling west .
We stayed in our own van but he location is superb  the amenities were clean and tidy. Reception is well organised and very helpful & friendly. We will definitely be back.

What a beautiful  camp-site. We stayed in cabin no.5
Best cabin we have stayed in so far in Brisbane
The staff were all so friendly and the grounds very clean and well kept.
We will definitely be back. 🩷

What a beatiful place to stay, right on the water.
Dinner at the Tavern was incredible and the Breakfast Burger @Black Drop Cafe was to die for. Thanks Pottsville.
Great Park in great location with very clean amenities. Can be a bit tight but take you time it's holidays.

Great smaller Park. Great location, near shops and inlet.
Lovely staff and neat and tidy facilities. A bit tight and always popular. Great foreshore seating and views.

Beautiful location on Mooball Creek. Not many caravan parks offer such a relaxing spot with a great view. The Indian Restaurant a short walk away was a bonus as was cycling the rail trail from Mooball to Stokers Crossing.

Fantastic caravan park although the sites are very tight, once your in the rest is such a great vibe. Anything you need is at hand. Beautiful grassed area to relax and watch the kids play in the sandy bay building sandcastles, kyacking,catching yabbies, fishing,and swimming. Good clean undercover bbq and seating. Beach access is a short walk through the scrub. Village Shops, cafes, bottle shop,iga, bait shop, club, footy oval, small hardware store are all across the road without the hustle and bustle and noise of the bigger shopping malls. I am middle aged and found This location is a true gem.

A great location for children
Would recommend it to anyone
Friendly staff
I stayed here to catch up with family but won't be back. Nice enough van park, but too expensive  for me.

We've holidayed here for 2 summer holidays. Nice park. Great for the kids to ride around and we love the pool. We like that they organise fun things for the kids in the holidays too. Amenities are always clean. Could do with an extra clothes dryer in the laundry.

Great family park, very affordable and within bike ride or short drive of shops, beaches & other activities.
Have visited multiple times and always been a safe park.
Only downside is lack of decent phone, particularly internet, reception. They do offer free wifi but its very unreliable and slow speed.

I stayed in garden cabin 1 night and tent site 6 nights
Tent site good but annoying people out back talking all night
Cabin was nice but had so many scratches and dents etc in the walls (it was garden cabin three)
Pool is good in summer but volleyball court gets all dirty and the basketball court had a big hole in backboard so there was kinda no point of it
All good service but office ladies can be a tiny bit fussy at times but I get they have hard job

Lovely stay at the Pottsville North holiday Park, amenities clean and quite good.
Stayed in the Safari Tent.
Extremely comfortable beds.
Camp kitchen was handy and well kept.
The sports club is a walk from the holiday park with great specials nightly.
Beach is lovely also.
If you're wanting to use your phone at all in pottsville enjoy a quiet time. The cell service is quite bad.
A lovely time away though.

My favorite place for my entire trip.will be booking here forvall my future trips.
Peaceful. Clean, friendly and you can walk to everything.
Loved pottsville.

Lovely caravan park. Clean amenities, great price, the trees near sites are great and it has a tranquil atmosphere, great for families, although a mixture of all people staying here. Pool is freezing though (heating in Sept would be great) and a little far away from the water for the young kiddos but most things within walking/biking/scooting distance. Got to get in the car to visit the different beaches anyway. Colour run for the kiddos a highlight!
Very clean, well kept sites.  Amenities were very clean.  Not far from shops.

We stayed at this fabulous park, absolutely quiet, tranquil, very clean, and generous caravan sites. Wonderful staff, very helpful and friendly. Cannot fault this park and totally recommend. Very short walk to town with amazing coffee shops. Wish we could stay longer. Well done, thank you.

We stayed at this fabulous park, absolutely quiet, tranquil, very clean, and generous caravan sites. Wonderful staff, very helpful and friendly. Cannot fault this park and totally recommend. Wish we could stay longer. Well done, thank you.

One of the most idyllic caravan parks you will encounter. Situated on Mooball Creek at Pottsville Nth NSW, amenities are very clean and accessible from any position in the park.

There are two well established camp kitchens which have clean barbeques, plenty of table space and washing up facilities.

An easy walk to the main beach across Mooball Creek. Turn right once on the beach and you have an easy stroll to Black Rock just south of Pottsville.

Sites on the park are  little tight but most caravaners are courtesy and will move vehicles to allow ease of parking.
Loved camping here.
Wow what a spot!  We just had our first family camping trip and it was amazing the location is absolutely perfect!  The staff are so lovely and helpful!  Can’t wait to book another trip!
Nice sites.  Waterfront.  Easy checking. Across road from shops. Large camp kitchens
Great location. Small sites

Bought 2 nights in a 2 bedroom cabin, got the third night free. On Mooball creek, great for swimming. Could see and hear the surf from our cabin. Fully equipped kitchen, full bathroom (shower only not bath) with second toilet. Affordable rates, however books up quickly so get in early. Park has a number of cabins, plus caravan, tent and motor home sites. 2 communal kitchens plus fish cleaning station. Walking tracks to beach. Short stroll to shops, restaurants, cafes, op shop etc. The weekend we were there, Pottsville markets where on at the adjacent vacant block. Short drive to Hastings Point or good adventure trek along the beach.
Powered site was great.
Fantastic caravan park. Excellent for swimming and relaxing. Great facilities and very friendly staff.
Just a great spot to totally relax & enjoy the charming small village without the crowds 😂😂👍👍
Stayed three nights wish it was more great place well looked after
All g/f  great food

What an amazing place. This was our first time here and we were unsure what to expect and only booked in for 3 nights, even though we could have stayed for 6. We were disappointed that we couldn't stay longer, as the site we was pre-booked. The park itself and all the amenities are spotless. A big thanks to Matt who keeps the park so clean and is always keen to help out and is so energetic. Thank you to all the staff at the front office who answered all our questions. IGA, the butcher and cafes are less than a two minute walk. We were that impressed with our stay, we have booked for a week next year.
Excellent park, very well run
Amazing setting in a great family oriented park. Good amenities and great staff. Would recommend. Cheers for having us team.

Motorhome site, fully powered with immaculate laundry and ablution facilities.
The beach was just superb and safe⛱️.
A very busy Park due to proximity to surf beach and clean tidal river.
A fabulous place for families.
Absolutely beautiful. Quite . Nice beach. Great coffee shop only across the road and the pub is just a short walk away.
Love camping here with the caravan , hate that's it's hard to get bookings even as a regular.

We had the best time. I had just finished building our camper and we stayed for a week. Was the best week we have ever had, enjoyed the water, park and town. Matt the maintenance dude was awesome, the girls were great which makes an awesome team.
We will be back for sure.
The park is a 5 out of 5 and would highly recommend this place.
Thanks for the experience guys.
Drew, Net & Blake Hocking

PS- the fish and chips over the road was so nice.
We camped, good site, clean toilets, but bloody windy our only complaint.

Stayed on powered caravan site.On the creek,was beautiful. Small,but,modern amenities block.Surf was close.Shops and eateries in walking distance. Very good size site for a coastal caravan park.Great small park.We loved it.Unfortunatley,its not dog friendly.But if you can get a dog sitter,we'll worth a visit

It is a great spot,showers are hot,very clean,so close to water.Very small park,such great view.Dogs aren't allowed,understand that with such a small park.Absolutley recommend. Unfortunately, we were advised to keep eskies ,valuables locked away.

Absolutely loved this delightful and surprising hidden gem. Staff were great we checked in late and they had all the comms to us to get in. Woke up overlooking the water and was straight out the rooftop tent and into the water for a swim! Clean amenities, close to everything in town. We’ll be back!
Great location and service. Close to shops and on the water front.

This was our first time here and must say what a beautiful stay we had. Spotless amenities and camp kitchens and a HUGE shout out to Matthew the maintenance man for doing such a wonderful job keeping the grounds looking so good, such a nice man to talk to and full of energy, deserves more money. Well done Matthew, we will be back.
We camped there and had water front and was really nice.
Great caravan park.  Close to shops, creek and beach.

Excellent park BUT. We stayed on an ensuite site for 10 days. On the 5th day I requested another toilet roll. To quote Seinfeld they didn’t have a “square to spare”. I said we’re staying 10 days. No, we only provide enough to get you started. |It is not a functioning ensuite if you need to use the camp facilities for toilet paper. |Come on guys, how mean can you be?|Jenny

I called expecting friendly staff but didnt get what i expected! I am disabled and wanted to book so that i can be near the amenaties. The woman had zero interest in helping and just said turn up on the day. Really poor service.
Peaceful and quiet. Clean amenities. Camp kitchen was spotless. Friendly and helpful staff. Very relaxing by the water. Easy walking to shops and the beach.
Great spot good size sites and really good and clean camp kitchen

Absolutely great.Cabin lovely,clean,nicely presented.Had everything needed.
Staff friendly.I have stayed in a lot of places...Bed so comfortable A1 .
Love this place , nice and clean and close to everything
